# tsb_gateway_install_gateway_template (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `gateway_spec` (Attributes) The gateway spec to apply to the created gateway. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--gateway_spec))
- `name` (String) Short name of the InstallGatewayTemplate resource.
- `parent` (String) The parent id for the InstallGatewayTemplate resource.

### Optional

- `all_clusters_selector` (Boolean) Selects all the onboarded clusters on TSB.
- `cluster_selector` (Attributes) Selects the gateways targeting the clusters based on cluster specific details (e.g. cluster name or labels). (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--cluster_selector))
- `deletion_protection_enabled` (Boolean) When set, prevents the resource from being deleted. In order to delete the resource this property needs to be set to `false` first.
- `description` (String) A description of the resource.
- `display_name` (String) User friendly name for the resource.
- `environment_selector` (Attributes) Selects the gateways targeting the clusters based on environment details, such as the provider. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--environment_selector))
- `gateway_workload_selector` (Attributes) Matches the workload selector defined by a Gateway configuration. Optional. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--gateway_workload_selector))
- `priority` (Number) Indicates when a template must be chosen in case of multiple selectors of the same type matching a single gateway configuration. Defaults to 0, the highest priority. When two templates have the same priority, they are sorted alphabetically by their names. Templates with different selector types will be resolved in the following order, regardless of the priority value: 1. environment selectors 2. cluster selectors with no namespace selectors 3. cluster selectors with namespace selector matching labels 4. cluster selectors with namespace selector matching name 5. specific InstallGateway TSB resources

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Fully-qualified name of the InstallGatewayTemplate resource.

<a id="nestedatt--gateway_spec"></a>
### Nested Schema for `gateway_spec`

Optional:

- `concurrency` (Number) Number of Envoy worker threads to run. By default, it will be set automatically based on the gateway's CPU resource limits. Set to `-1` to use the legacy behavior of all cores on the machine.
- `connection_drain_duration` (Attributes) The amount of time the gateway will wait on shutdown for connections to complete before terminating the gateway. During this drain period, no new connections can be created but existing ones are allowed to complete. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--gateway_spec--connection_drain_duration))
- `kube_spec` (Attributes) Configure Kubernetes specific settings. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--gateway_spec--kube_spec))
- `revision` (String) Specifies the Istio revision to reconcile with. If specified, the TSB Control Plane operator will reconcile this gateway only if the operator's revision matches the revision. The TSB Data Plane operator, which runs only when the TSB Control Plane operator has not configured a revision, will ignore the revision and will reconcile the gateway as usual. Internally, the revision specifies which Istio Control Plane configures the Gateway deployment. See https://istio.io/latest/docs/setup/upgrade/canary.
- `type` (String) Type defines the type of gateway deployment created as part of this gateway install object. Possible values are UNIFIED, INGRESS, EGRESS and EASTWEST.

<a id="nestedatt--gateway_spec--connection_drain_duration"></a>
### Nested Schema for `gateway_spec.connection_drain_duration`

Optional:

- `nanos` (Number) Signed fractions of a second at nanosecond resolution of the span of time. Durations less than one second are represented with a 0 `seconds` field and a positive or negative `nanos` field. For durations of one second or more, a non-zero value for the `nanos` field must be of the same sign as the `seconds` field. Must be from -999,999,999 to +999,999,999 inclusive.
- `seconds` (Number) Signed seconds of the span of time. Must be from -315,576,000,000 to +315,576,000,000 inclusive. Note: these bounds are computed from: 60 sec/min * 60 min/hr * 24 hr/day * 365.25 days/year * 10000 years


<a id="nestedatt--gateway_spec--kube_spec"></a>
### Nested Schema for `gateway_spec.kube_spec`

Optional:

- `deployment` (Attributes) Settings related to the component deployment (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--gateway_spec--kube_spec--deployment))
- `overlays` (Attributes List) Post-render overlays to mutate Kubernetes manifests https://istio.io/latest/docs/reference/config/istio.operator.v1alpha1/#K8sObjectOverlay (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--gateway_spec--kube_spec--overlays))
- `service` (Attributes) Settings related to the component service (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--gateway_spec--kube_spec--service))
- `service_account` (Attributes) Settings related to the component service account (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--gateway_spec--kube_spec--service_account))

<a id="nestedatt--gateway_spec--kube_spec--deployment"></a>
### Nested Schema for `gateway_spec.kube_spec.deployment`

Optional:

- `affinity` (Attributes) The scheduling constraints for the pod. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/assign-pod-node/#affinity-and-anti-affinity (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--gateway_spec--kube_spec--deployment--affinity))
- `container_security_context` (Attributes) k8s container security context [Set the security context for a Container](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-container) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--gateway_spec--kube_spec--deployment--container_security_context))
- `env` (Attributes List) Environment variables for all containers in the deployment. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/inject-data-application/define-environment-variable-container/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--gateway_spec--kube_spec--deployment--env))
- `hpa_spec` (Attributes) Horizontal Pod Autoscaler automatically scales the number of pods in a deployment based on a specified metric. Kubernetes periodically adjusts the number of replicas in a deployment to match the observed metric to the target specified. The version of Horizontal Pod Autoscaler currently used is `[v2beta1](https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#horizontalpodautoscaler-v2beta1-autoscaling)`. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/run-application/horizontal-pod-autoscale/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--gateway_spec--kube_spec--deployment--hpa_spec))
- `labels` (Map of String) Labels are an unstructured key-value map stored with the Deployment. For Gateway deployments, these labels are propagated to the Pod, Service, etc. For more details: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels/
- `pod_annotations` (Map of String) Pod annotations are an unstructured key-value map stored with the Deployment's Pod template. These annotations will be added to the Pod template within the Deployment. For more details: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations/
- `pod_security_context` (Attributes) k8s pod security context [Set the security context for a Pod](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/security-context/#set-the-security-context-for-a-pod) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--gateway_spec--kube_spec--deployment--pod_security_context))
- `priority_class_name` (String) If specified, indicates the pod's priority class. The value must match the name of a PriorityClass object that already exists in the cluster. If not specified, the pod's priority will be default or zero if there is no default. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/scheduling-eviction/pod-priority-preemption/
- `replica_count` (Number) Number of desired pods. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentspec-v1-apps
- `resources` (Attributes) Compute Resources required by the primary container in the deployment PodSpec. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-compute-resources-container/#resource-requests-and-limits-of-pod-and-container (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--gateway_spec--kube_spec--deployment--resources))
- `strategy` (Attributes) The deployment strategy to use to replace existing pods with new ones. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#deploymentstrategy-v1-apps (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--gateway_spec--kube_spec--deployment--strategy))
- `tolerations` (Attributes List) Tolerations are applied to pods, and allow (but do not require) the pods to schedule onto nodes with matching taints. Taints and tolerations work together to ensure that pods are not scheduled onto inappropriate nodes. One or more taints are applied to a node; this marks that the node should not accept any pods that do not tolerate the taints. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/taint-and-toleration/ (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--gateway_spec--kube_spec--deployment--tolerations))

<a id="nestedatt--gateway_spec--kube_spec--deployment--affinity"></a>
### Nested Schema for `gateway_spec.kube_spec.deployment.affinity`

Optional:

- `node_affinity` (Attributes) Group of node affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#nodeaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--gateway_spec--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity))
- `pod_affinity` (Attributes) Group of inter-pod affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#podaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--gateway_spec--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--pod_affinity))
- `pod_anti_affinity` (Attributes) Group of inter-pod anti-affinity scheduling rules. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#podantiaffinity-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--gateway_spec--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--pod_anti_affinity))

Optional:

- `preferred_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution` (Attributes List) The scheduler will prefer to schedule pods to nodes that satisfy the affinity expressions specified by this field, but it may choose a node that violates one or more of the expressions. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#preferredschedulingterm-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--gateway_spec--kube_spec--deployment--affinity--node_affinity--preferred_during_scheduling_ignored_during_execution))

<a id="nestedatt--gateway_spec--kube_spec--overlays"></a>
### Nested Schema for `gateway_spec.kube_spec.overlays`

Optional:

- `api_version` (String) Resource API version.
- `kind` (String) Resource kind.
- `name` (String) Name of resource. Namespace is always the component namespace.
- `patches` (Attributes List) List of patches to apply to resource. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--gateway_spec--kube_spec--overlays--patches))

<a id="nestedatt--gateway_spec--kube_spec--overlays--patches"></a>
### Nested Schema for `gateway_spec.kube_spec.overlays.patches`

Optional:

- `path` (String) Path of the form a.[key1:value1].b.[:value2] Where [key1:value1] is a selector for a key-value pair to identify a list element and [:value] is a value selector to identify a list element in a leaf list. All path intermediate nodes must exist.
- `value` (String) Value to add, delete or replace. For add, the path should be a new leaf. For delete, value should be unset. For replace, path should reference an existing node. All values are strings but are converted into appropriate type based on schema.



<a id="nestedatt--gateway_spec--kube_spec--service"></a>
### Nested Schema for `gateway_spec.kube_spec.service`

Optional:

- `annotations` (Map of String) Pod annotations are an unstructured key value map stored with the service. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/annotations/
- `labels` (Map of String) Labels are an unstructured key value map stored with the deployment. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/labels
- `ports` (Attributes List) List of ports exposed by the component's service. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#serviceport-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--gateway_spec--kube_spec--service--ports))
- `type` (String) Determines how the Service is exposed. Valid options are ExternalName, ClusterIP, NodePort, and LoadBalancer. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/services-networking/service/#publishing-services-service-types

<a id="nestedatt--gateway_spec--kube_spec--service--ports"></a>
### Nested Schema for `gateway_spec.kube_spec.service.ports`

- `name` (String)
- `node_port` (Number)
- `port` (Number)
- `protocol` (String)
- `target_port` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--gateway_spec--kube_spec--service--ports--target_port))

<a id="nestedatt--gateway_spec--kube_spec--service_account"></a>
### Nested Schema for `gateway_spec.kube_spec.service_account`

Optional:

- `image_pull_secrets` (Attributes List) List of references to secrets in the same namespace to use for pulling any images in pods that reference this ServiceAccount. ImagePullSecrets are distinct from Secrets because Secrets can be mounted in the pod, but ImagePullSecrets are only accessed by the kubelet. More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/containers/images/#specifying-imagepullsecrets-on-a-pod https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/generated/kubernetes-api/v1.18/#service_account-v1-core (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--gateway_spec--kube_spec--service_account--image_pull_secrets))

### Nested Schema for `gateway_spec.kube_spec.service_account.image_pull_secrets`

Optional:

- `name` (String) Name of the referent.





<a id="nestedatt--cluster_selector"></a>
### Nested Schema for `cluster_selector`

Optional:

- `labels_selector` (Attributes) Matches the minimum required set of labels defined in the cluster metadata. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--cluster_selector--labels_selector))
- `name` (String) Matches the name of the cluster.
- `namespace_selector` (Attributes List) Selector to target namespaces in the matching cluster. When empty, any Cluster namespace is matched. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--cluster_selector--namespace_selector))

<a id="nestedatt--cluster_selector--labels_selector"></a>
### Nested Schema for `cluster_selector.labels_selector`

- `name` (String) The namespace name.

Optional:

- `labels` (Map of String)
